title: arrhenatherum elatius f. striatum
intro: Arrhenatherum elatius f. striatum, also known as the meadow oat-grass or striated false oat, is a variety of the tall oat-grass (Arrhenatherum elatius) that belongs to the grass family (Poaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Arrhenatherum elatium f. striatum include reducing inflammation and promoting healing, which can be beneficial for conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and can be found in some herbal remedies for skin and wound care.
